pull.title: [Perf] SM100 - add swap AB optimization to CUTLASS FP8 GEMM
pull.body: ## Purpose 1. Added swap AB optimization to CUTLASS FP8 GEMM on B200 (SM100) to improve performance for small batch size - M <=64 cases 2. Tuned kernel configs by leveraging nvMatmulHeuristics **Context on Swap-AB Optimization** For the 5th-generation Tensor Core (tcgen05.mma) instruction on SM100, the supported matrix shapes include 64 × N × 16 (with N a multiple of 8) and 128 × N × 16 (with N a multiple of 16), where M is fixed at 64 or 128. When the batch dimension M is small (<=64) , it can be beneficial to swap M and N to reduce padding overhead, since it is typically easier to ensure that N aligns with these required multiples. Similar optimization done on H200: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/20396/ ## Test Plan Performance: used vllm/benchmarks/kernels/bench_fp8_gemm.py to test problem spaces for Llama 450B and Llama 70B Accuracy: CUTLASS FP8 GEMM unit tests in tests/kernels/quantization/test_cutlass_scaled_mm.py ## Test Result **Accuracy: confirmed the following CUTLASS FP8 GEMM unit tests all passed:** tests/kernels/quantization/test_cutlass_scaled_mm.py::test_cutlass_fp8_gemm ======================= 152 passed, 1 warning in 32.53s ======================== tests/kernels/quantization/test_cutlass_scaled_mm.py::test_cutlass_fp8_gemm_m_sweep =================== 8 passed, 1 warning in 234.94s (0:03:54) =================== **Performance: 5% to 25% improvement across different M/N/K and quantization schemes for Llama 450B (tests performed with 8 B200 GPUs with TP8)** Detailed results for Llama 450B:
